summary: Agent Role Builder role frozen successfully
status_reason:
Frozen. The remaining material issues from the prior round are resolved: the authority chain no longer duplicates the runtime review contract, `frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` are now mutually exclusive, and the required run/cycle postmortem artifacts are integrated into steps, outputs, and completion. The reviewer returned only a minor residual risk about future self-check coverage, with no blocking or major unresolved findings.
Governance override: Leader returned frozen while non-material repair work remained. Clean freeze is only legal when no repair work remains.

participant_records.verdict: {"verdict":"reject","conceptual_groups":[{"id":"group-1","summary":"Terminal states are not source-faithful or mutually exclusive.","severity":"blocking","findings":[{"id":"f1","description":"`pushback` is defined as a terminal state when reviewers still reject and review budget remains, but the source review loop says a reject verdict triggers learning, fix, and re-review. That makes `pushback` overlap with the normal in-progress reject path instead of a disjoint terminal predicate.","source_section":"<steps> Step 4e and Step 5 (`pushback`); <completion> `pushback`"},{"id":"f2","description":"`blocked` is triggered by a generic 'non-recoverable validation or structural error' without encoding the required error-escalation sequence from the authority docs: stop, write bug report, attempt auto-fix/relaunch, then only exit `blocked` if the fix fails or the error persists. The current predicate is not mechanically testable against that lifecycle.","source_section":"<steps> Step 5 (`blocked`); <completion> `blocked`"}],"redesign_guidance":"Keep review rejection inside the Step 4 revision loop. Reserve terminal states for outcomes that actually end the run. If `pushback` must exist, define it only for request/input ambiguity with a disjoint trigger and artifact set. Make `blocked` explicitly depend on failed error-escalation or auto-fix, not on the initial appearance of an error."},{"id":"group-2","summary":"Arbitration mechanics add unsupported semantics and blur contract-owned details into the role text.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f3","description":"The arbitration trigger 'split verdicts persist for 2 or more consecutive rounds after revision' is presented as derived from the review-process architecture, but that condition does not appear in the cited authority. The source only allows leader arbitration for reviewer disagreement on minor or suggestion items.","source_section":"<guardrails> Arbitration; <steps> Step 4f"},{"id":"f4","description":"The role hardcodes `result.json` fields `arbitration_used=true` and `arbitration_rationale` as required evidence even though the source authority only requires rationale logging and a `frozen_with_conditions` result listing deferred minor items. Field-level audit schema belongs in contracts, so this is not source-grounded as written.","source_section":"<guardrails> Arbitration; <steps> Step 5 (`frozen_with_conditions`); <completion> `frozen_with_conditions`"}],"redesign_guidance":"Use only source-backed arbitration rules in the markdown role: minor or suggestion disagreement, leader decides, no override of blocking or major items or reject verdicts, outcome is `frozen_with_conditions`. If extra trigger thresholds or result fields are true contract requirements, cite the runtime review contract as the authority instead of claiming they come from the architecture docs."},{"id":"group-3","summary":"Cross-round finding tracking is keyed at the conceptual-group level, which is too coarse for mechanical carry-forward.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f5","description":"The fix items map is keyed by `conceptual_group` ID even though the review process requires handling each reviewer finding. A single conceptual group can contain multiple findings, so group-level IDs cannot preserve accept or reject status or carry-forward per finding.","source_section":"<steps> Step 4b"},{"id":"f6","description":"The reviewer fix decision map repeats the same coarse keying by `conceptual_group` ID, which makes `accept_fix`, `reject_fix`, `accept_rejection`, and `reject_rejection` ambiguous whenever more than one finding exists in the group.","source_section":"<steps> Step 4c"}],"redesign_guidance":"Assign stable IDs to individual findings and carry them across rounds. Keep conceptual-group IDs for grouping and root-cause presentation only; use finding IDs for fix maps, reviewer decision maps, and explicit carried-forward unresolved items."}],"fix_decisions":[],"residual_risks":["The current self-check explicitly leaves lifecycle consistency and cross-section semantic coherence as target-state checks, so these governance errors would not be caught mechanically before review.","Some contract-owned runtime details are still duplicated in markdown instead of being sourced from the runtime review contract, which creates drift risk if the contract changes."],"strengths":["The authority section cleanly separates operative precedence from reference evidence.","Canonical artifact promotion versus run-scoped evidence is clearly separated, and decision-log preservation is explicitly defined.","Context gathering is correctly treated as a precondition, and the self-check distinguishes current implementation checks from target-state checks."]}

{
  "status": "pushback",
  "rationale": "Pushback is still required. The artifact has real structural improvements, but it still diverges from the source authority on terminal-state closeout, arbitration mechanics, and per-finding carry-forward. Those are material governance gaps, so the self-check pass is not enough to freeze.",
  "unresolved": [
    "group-1: The terminal-state/closeout model is not source-faithful. It introduces pushback as a terminal state, while the authority docs define freeze/blocked/resume_required closeout flow, and it omits required run-postmortem.json and cycle-postmortem.json artifacts from outputs/completion.",
    "group-2: Arbitration mechanics still add unsupported semantics. The source authority allows leader arbitration only for minor/suggestion disagreements with logged rationale and frozen_with_conditions outcome; the artifact adds a 2+ consecutive split-round trigger and specific result.json field requirements that are not stated there.",
    "group-3: Cross-round tracking is still too coarse. Step 4b/4c keys fix handling by conceptual_group, but the review-process authority requires fix-items handling for each reviewer finding, so carry-forward is not mechanical at the finding level."
  ],
  "improvements_applied": [
    "Authority is now a strict operative precedence chain, with architecture/process docs clearly marked as reference evidence rather than binding authority.",
    "Canonical artifacts and run-scoped evidence are separated cleanly, with full tool-relative canonical paths for the promoted package files.",
    "Context gathering is correctly treated as a precondition block instead of a numbered execution step.",
    "Decision-history preservation is explicit: decision-log append behavior and board-summary replacement behavior are both defined.",
    "The self-check section now distinguishes current validator behavior from target-state checks instead of claiming unimplemented guarantees."
  ],
  "arbitration_used": false,
  "arbitration_rationale": null
}

{"verdict":"reject","conceptual_groups":[{"id":"group-1","summary":"The authority chain still duplicates the runtime review contract across two precedence levels.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f1","description":"`tools/agent-role-builder/review-contract.json` is declared as the active runtime review contract in authority item 2, then repeated again inside authority item 3 as a lower-precedence component-local governance file. That breaks the canonical layer split and makes the same artifact both superior and subordinate authority.","source_section":"<authority>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Keep the runtime review contract in one canonical authority slot only. List rulebook and review-prompt as required loaded governance files separately, or keep a load list outside the precedence chain. Do not restate the same review-contract file as a lower-precedence authority after already elevating it as the contract layer."},{"id":"group-2","summary":"`frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` still overlap, so closeout predicates are not mutually exclusive.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f2","description":"The `frozen` trigger does not exclude arbitration, while `frozen_with_conditions` is defined as the `frozen` threshold plus arbitration. When arbitration is used, both predicates are true, which conflicts with the completion clause requiring exactly one terminal closeout path.","source_section":"<guardrails> Canonical definitions; <steps> Step 5 (`frozen`, `frozen_with_conditions`); <completion>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Define `frozen` as the clean freeze path with no arbitration used, and define `frozen_with_conditions` as the same threshold plus arbitration. Mirror that exclusion in guardrails, Step 5, and completion so only one branch can fire."}],"fix_decisions":[{"finding_group_id":"group-1-summary","decision":"reject_fix","reason":"The prior terminal-state fix resolved the old pushback/review-loop problem, but terminal closeout is still not fully correct because `frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` remain overlapping predicates."},{"finding_group_id":"group-2-summary","decision":"accept_fix","reason":"The unsupported multi-round arbitration threshold was removed, and arbitration evidence is now delegated to runtime-contract-governed run evidence instead of invented local fields."},{"finding_group_id":"group-3-summary","decision":"accept_fix","reason":"Step 4b and Step 4c now track and review findings by stable `finding_id`, with `conceptual_group_id` retained only for grouping context."},{"finding_group_id":"group-4-summary","decision":"accept_fix","reason":"Decision-log preservation and prior board-summary preservation now have explicit run-directory snapshot paths and write timing."},{"finding_group_id":"group-5-summary","decision":"accept_fix","reason":"Step 1 now routes structural validation failures mechanically to `blocked` and semantically insufficient inputs to pre-review pushback before drafting."}],"residual_risks":["The self-check still treats lifecycle consistency and cross-section semantic coherence as target-state-only checks, so the remaining authority and closeout drift would not be caught mechanically before review.","`All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed` remains a local rule without support in the cited authority files; unless it is explicitly owned by the runtime review contract, it risks reintroducing unsupported governance semantics."],"strengths":["Pre-review pushback is now separated from review terminal closeout, which fixes the earlier loop-versus-terminal confusion.","Cross-round carry-forward now uses stable finding IDs, which makes fix tracking and reviewer decisions mechanically auditable.","Decision-history preservation is materially stronger: both the decision-log snapshot and prior board-summary snapshot are now explicit run-scoped artifacts with concrete paths."]}

{
  "status": "pushback",
  "rationale": "Pushback remains. This revision fixes the earlier arbitration and per-finding carry-forward issues, but there are still material source-authority gaps: the authority chain duplicates the runtime review contract across two precedence levels, the `frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` predicates still overlap instead of being mutually exclusive, and the source-defined `run-postmortem.json` and `cycle-postmortem.json` artifacts are still missing from outputs and closeout.",
  "unresolved": [
    "The authority model still duplicates `tools/agent-role-builder/review-contract.json` across precedence level 2 and the component-local governance list at level 3, so the contract is not canonically defined once in the authority chain.",
    "`frozen` and `frozen_with_conditions` still overlap: `frozen_with_conditions` is triggered when the `frozen` threshold is met plus arbitration, but `frozen` does not exclude arbitration, so the closeout predicates are not mutually exclusive.",
    "The artifact lifecycle is still incomplete against the source authority because `run-postmortem.json` and `cycle-postmortem.json` are not declared in `<outputs>` or required in closeout even though the authority docs define them as governed review artifacts."
  ],
  "improvements_applied": [
    "Pre-review pushback is now separated from review terminal closeout, which removes the earlier pushback-as-terminal ambiguity.",
    "Arbitration was rewritten to source-backed semantics only: minor or suggestion disagreements, leader-decided, no override of reject or blocking/major findings, and closeout only as `frozen_with_conditions`.",
    "Cross-round tracking now uses stable `finding_id` handling, with `conceptual_group_id` retained only for grouping and severity context.",
    "Decision-history preservation is stronger: the decision-log snapshot path and prior board-summary snapshot path are both explicit run-scoped artifacts.",
    "Step 1 failure routing is now mechanical: structural validation failures go to `blocked`, while semantically insufficient inputs stop in pre-review pushback."
  ],
  "arbitration_used": false,
  "arbitration_rationale": null
}

{"verdict":"conditional","conceptual_groups":[{"id":"group-1","summary":"One unsupported local guardrail remains in the draft.","severity":"minor","findings":[{"id":"f1","description":"`All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed` is asserted as an execution guardrail, but that requirement is not present in the cited source authority files or the request contract summary. Because the draft otherwise says local governance mechanics should be inherited rather than invented, this line reintroduces an unsourced rule.","source_section":"<guardrails> Execution guardrails"}],"redesign_guidance":"Remove the slug-prefix rule from the markdown role, or explicitly root it in the active runtime review contract if that contract is the real authority for it.
